Suez Canal Transits Analysis

Source: the datasets to build the analysis have been downloaded from the IMF PortWatch website, accessible through this link: "https://portwatch.imf.org/pages/c57c79bf612b4372b08a9c6ea9c97ef0".
The graphs created have been inspired directly from the PortWatch dashboard; the code is instead personally developed. The analysis itself has been created following personal criteria developed during the thesis' scientific research.

Graph 1 — Arrivals of Ships

Original interactive chart from notebook cell 0.

—
Container Dry Bulk General Cargo Roll-on/roll-off Tanker 7-day Moving Average Prior Year: 7-day Moving Average

Graph 2 — Arrivals of Ships, refined layout

Refined interactive chart from notebook cell 2.

—
Container Dry Bulk General Cargo Roll-on/roll-off Tanker 7-day Moving Average Prior Year: 7-day Moving Average

Graph 3 — Metric Tons by vessel category

Interactive stacked chart from notebook cell 3.

—
Container Dry Bulk General Cargo Roll-on/roll-off Tanker 7-day Moving Average Prior Year: 7-day Moving Average

Script Python

Cell 0 - Python code
import pandas as pd
import plotly.graph_objects as go

import pandas as pd
import plotly.graph_objects as go
from pathlib import Path

csv_path = Path(r"C:\Users\MaccagnanG\Downloads\arrivals-of-ships.csv")

df = pd.read_csv(csv_path, sep=";", decimal=",")
df["DateTime"] = pd.to_datetime(df["DateTime"])

categories = [
    "Container",
    "Dry Bulk",
    "General Cargo",
    "Roll-on/roll-off",
    "Tanker"
]

colors = {
    "Container": "#d73027",
    "Dry Bulk": "#fc8d59",
    "General Cargo": "#fee08b",
    "Roll-on/roll-off": "#91bfdb",
    "Tanker": "#0b4f27",
    "7-day Moving Average": "#f39c12",
    "Prior Year: 7-day Moving Average": "#4d4d4d",
}

fig = go.Figure()

for col in categories:
    fig.add_trace(
        go.Scatter(
            x=df["DateTime"],
            y=df[col],
            name=col,
            mode="lines",
            line=dict(width=0.3, color=colors[col]),
            stackgroup="ships"
        )
    )

fig.add_trace(
    go.Scatter(
        x=df["DateTime"],
        y=df["7-day Moving Average"],
        name="7-day Moving Average",
        mode="lines",
        line=dict(color=colors["7-day Moving Average"], width=1.4)
    )
)

fig.add_trace(
    go.Scatter(
        x=df["DateTime"],
        y=df["Prior Year: 7-day Moving Average"],
        name="Prior Year: 7-day Moving Average",
        mode="lines",
        line=dict(
            color=colors["Prior Year: 7-day Moving Average"],
            width=1.3,
            dash="dash"
        )
    )
)

fig.update_layout(
    title=dict(text="<b>Arrivals of Ships</b>", x=0.5),
    template="plotly_white",
    width=1900,
    height=520,
    margin=dict(l=75, r=25, t=70, b=95),
    hovermode="x unified",
    legend=dict(
        orientation="h",
        yanchor="top",
        y=-0.24,
        xanchor="center",
        x=0.5
    ),
    annotations=[
        dict(
            text="Sources: UN Global Platform; PortWatch.",
            x=1,
            y=-0.38,
            xref="paper",
            yref="paper",
            showarrow=False,
            font=dict(size=10),
            xanchor="right"
        )
    ]
)

fig.update_yaxes(
    title_text="Number of Ships",
    range=[0, 58],
    dtick=20,
    showgrid=False
)

fig.update_xaxes(
    tickformat="%b %Y",
    dtick="M6",
    rangeslider=dict(visible=True, thickness=0.09),
    rangeselector=dict(
        buttons=[
            dict(count=1, label="1m", step="month", stepmode="backward"),
            dict(count=3, label="3m", step="month", stepmode="backward"),
            dict(count=6, label="6m", step="month", stepmode="backward"),
            dict(count=1, label="YTD", step="year", stepmode="todate"),
            dict(count=1, label="1y", step="year", stepmode="backward"),
            dict(step="all", label="All"),
        ]
    )
)

fig.write_html("arrivals_of_ships_ricreato.html")
fig.show()
Cell 1 - Python code
from pathlib import Path

csv_path = Path(r"C:\Users\MaccagnanG\Downloads\arrivals-of-ships.csv")

print(csv_path.exists())
Cell 2 - Python code
import pandas as pd
import plotly.graph_objects as go
from pathlib import Path

# ============================================================
# 1. Percorso del file CSV
# ============================================================

csv_path = Path(r"C:\Users\MaccagnanG\Downloads\arrivals-of-ships.csv")

if not csv_path.exists():
    raise FileNotFoundError(
        f"File non trovato:\n{csv_path}\n\n"
        "Controlla che il file sia davvero nella cartella Downloads "
        "e che il nome sia esattamente arrivals-of-ships.csv"
    )

# ============================================================
# 2. Lettura del dataset
# ============================================================

df = pd.read_csv(
    csv_path,
    sep=";",
    decimal=","
)

df["DateTime"] = pd.to_datetime(df["DateTime"])

# ============================================================
# 3. Colonne da rappresentare
# ============================================================

categories = [
    "Container",
    "Dry Bulk",
    "General Cargo",
    "Roll-on/roll-off",
    "Tanker"
]

colors = {
    "Container": "#d73027",
    "Dry Bulk": "#fc8d59",
    "General Cargo": "#fee08b",
    "Roll-on/roll-off": "#91bfdb",
    "Tanker": "#0b4f27",
    "7-day Moving Average": "#f39c12",
    "Prior Year: 7-day Moving Average": "#4d4d4d",
}

# ============================================================
# 4. Creazione del grafico
# ============================================================

fig = go.Figure()

# Area stacked per tipologia di nave
for col in categories:
    fig.add_trace(
        go.Scatter(
            x=df["DateTime"],
            y=df[col],
            name=col,
            mode="lines",
            line=dict(
                width=0.3,
                color=colors[col]
            ),
            stackgroup="ships"
        )
    )

# Linea media mobile a 7 giorni
fig.add_trace(
    go.Scatter(
        x=df["DateTime"],
        y=df["7-day Moving Average"],
        name="7-day Moving Average",
        mode="lines",
        line=dict(
            color=colors["7-day Moving Average"],
            width=1.5
        )
    )
)

# Linea media mobile anno precedente
fig.add_trace(
    go.Scatter(
        x=df["DateTime"],
        y=df["Prior Year: 7-day Moving Average"],
        name="Prior Year: 7-day Moving Average",
        mode="lines",
        line=dict(
            color=colors["Prior Year: 7-day Moving Average"],
            width=1.3,
            dash="dash"
        )
    )
)

# ============================================================
# 5. Layout generale
# ============================================================

fig.update_layout(
    title=dict(
        text="<b>Arrivals of Ships</b>",
        x=0.5,
        y=0.96,
        font=dict(size=12)
    ),
    template="plotly_white",
    width=1900,
    height=540,
    margin=dict(
        l=75,
        r=35,
        t=105,
        b=110
    ),
    hovermode="x unified",
    legend=dict(
        orientation="h",
        yanchor="top",
        y=-0.24,
        xanchor="center",
        x=0.5,
        font=dict(size=12)
    ),
    annotations=[
        dict(
            text="Sources: UN Global Platform; PortWatch.",
            x=1,
            y=-0.40,
            xref="paper",
            yref="paper",
            showarrow=False,
            font=dict(size=12),
            xanchor="right"
        )
    ]
)

# ============================================================
# 6. Asse Y
# ============================================================

fig.update_yaxes(
    title_text="Number of Ships",
    range=[0, 58],
    dtick=20,
    showgrid=False,
    zeroline=False
)

# ============================================================
# 7. Asse X, pulsanti periodo e range slider
# ============================================================

fig.update_xaxes(
    tickformat="%b %Y",
    dtick="M6",
    showgrid=False,
    rangeslider=dict(
        visible=True,
        thickness=0.09
    ),
    rangeselector=dict(
        x=0,
        y=1.15,
        xanchor="left",
        yanchor="top",
        buttons=[
            dict(
                count=1,
                label="1m",
                step="month",
                stepmode="backward"
            ),
            dict(
                count=3,
                label="3m",
                step="month",
                stepmode="backward"
            ),
            dict(
                count=6,
                label="6m",
                step="month",
                stepmode="backward"
            ),
            dict(
                count=1,
                label="YTD",
                step="year",
                stepmode="todate"
            ),
            dict(
                count=1,
                label="1y",
                step="year",
                stepmode="backward"
            ),
            dict(
                step="all",
                label="All"
            )
        ]
    )
)

# ============================================================
# 8. Configurazione icone Plotly
#    Questa parte impedisce alla barra delle icone di sparire
# ============================================================

config = {
    "displayModeBar": True,
    "displaylogo": False,
    "responsive": True,
    "modeBarButtonsToAdd": [
        "resetScale2d"
    ]
}

# ============================================================
# 9. Salvataggio e visualizzazione
# ============================================================

output_path = Path(r"C:\Users\MaccagnanG\Downloads\arrivals_of_ships_ricreato.html")

fig.write_html(
    output_path,
    config=config
)

fig.show(config=config)

print(f"Grafico salvato in:\n{output_path}")
Cell 3 - Python code
import pandas as pd
import plotly.graph_objects as go
from pathlib import Path

# ============================================================
# 1. Percorso del file CSV
# ============================================================

csv_path = Path(r"C:\Users\MaccagnanG\Downloads\chart.csv")

if not csv_path.exists():
    raise FileNotFoundError(
        f"File non trovato:\n{csv_path}\n\n"
        "Controlla che il file sia nella cartella Downloads "
        "e che il nome sia esattamente chart.csv"
    )

# ============================================================
# 2. Lettura del dataset
# ============================================================

df = pd.read_csv(
    csv_path,
    sep=";",
    decimal=","
)

df["DateTime"] = pd.to_datetime(df["DateTime"])
df = df.sort_values("DateTime")

# ============================================================
# 3. Colonne da rappresentare
# ============================================================

categories = [
    "Container",
    "Dry Bulk",
    "General Cargo",
    "Roll-on/roll-off",
    "Tanker"
]

colors = {
    "Container": "#d73027",
    "Dry Bulk": "#fc8d59",
    "General Cargo": "#fee08b",
    "Roll-on/roll-off": "#91bfdb",
    "Tanker": "#145a32",
    "7-day Moving Average": "#f2a900",
    "Prior Year: 7-day Moving Average": "#4d4d4d",
}

# ============================================================
# 4. Intervallo iniziale visibile
#    Uguale alla schermata: circa 17 febbraio - 17 maggio 2026
# ============================================================

start_date = pd.Timestamp("2026-02-17")
end_date = pd.Timestamp("2026-05-17")

# Se per sicurezza le date non fossero nel dataset,
# vengono sostituite con gli estremi disponibili
if start_date < df["DateTime"].min():
    start_date = df["DateTime"].min()

if end_date > df["DateTime"].max():
    end_date = df["DateTime"].max()

# Tick settimanali dell'asse X principale
weekly_ticks = pd.date_range(
    start=start_date,
    end=end_date,
    freq="7D"
)

# ============================================================
# 5. Creazione del grafico
# ============================================================

fig = go.Figure()

# Barre stacked
for col in categories:
    fig.add_trace(
        go.Bar(
            x=df["DateTime"],
            y=df[col],
            name=col,
            marker=dict(
                color=colors[col],
                line=dict(width=0)
            ),
            hovertemplate=(
                "<b>%{x|%d %b %Y}</b><br>"
                + col +
                ": %{y:,.0f}<extra></extra>"
            )
        )
    )

# Linea 7-day Moving Average
fig.add_trace(
    go.Scatter(
        x=df["DateTime"],
        y=df["7-day Moving Average"],
        name="7-day Moving Average",
        mode="lines",
        line=dict(
            color=colors["7-day Moving Average"],
            width=1.6
        ),
        hovertemplate=(
            "<b>%{x|%d %b %Y}</b><br>"
            "7-day Moving Average: %{y:,.0f}<extra></extra>"
        )
    )
)

# Linea Prior Year
fig.add_trace(
    go.Scatter(
        x=df["DateTime"],
        y=df["Prior Year: 7-day Moving Average"],
        name="Prior Year: 7-day Moving Average",
        mode="lines",
        line=dict(
            color=colors["Prior Year: 7-day Moving Average"],
            width=1.4,
            dash="dash"
        ),
        hovertemplate=(
            "<b>%{x|%d %b %Y}</b><br>"
            "Prior Year: 7-day Moving Average: %{y:,.0f}<extra></extra>"
        )
    )
)

# ============================================================
# 6. Layout generale
# ============================================================

fig.update_layout(
    template="plotly_white",
    barmode="stack",
    bargap=0.25,

    width=1900,
    height=520,

    margin=dict(
        l=85,
        r=35,
        t=65,
        b=110
    ),

    hovermode="x unified",

    legend=dict(
        orientation="h",
        yanchor="top",
        y=-0.24,
        xanchor="center",
        x=0.5,
        font=dict(size=12),
        traceorder="normal"
    ),

    annotations=[
        dict(
            text="Sources: UN Global Platform; PortWatch.",
            x=1,
            y=-0.34,
            xref="paper",
            yref="paper",
            showarrow=False,
            font=dict(size=12),
            xanchor="right"
        )
    ]
)

# ============================================================
# 7. Asse Y
# ============================================================

fig.update_yaxes(
    title_text="Metric Tons",
    range=[0, 1450000],
    dtick=500000,
    tickformat="~s",
    showgrid=True,
    gridcolor="rgba(0,0,0,0.10)",
    zeroline=False
)

# ============================================================
# 8. Asse X
#    Qui viene eliminato il problema dello 01 del mese.
#    Non si usa più dtick='M1'.
# ============================================================

fig.update_xaxes(
    range=[start_date, end_date],

    tickmode="array",
    tickvals=weekly_ticks,
    tickformat="%-d %b",

    showgrid=False,
    zeroline=False,

    rangeslider=dict(
        visible=True,
        thickness=0.10,
        bgcolor="rgba(230,230,230,0.35)",
        bordercolor="rgba(160,160,160,0.5)",
        borderwidth=1
    ),

    rangeselector=dict(
        x=0,
        y=1.12,
        xanchor="left",
        yanchor="top",
        buttons=[
            dict(
                count=1,
                label="1m",
                step="month",
                stepmode="backward"
            ),
            dict(
                count=3,
                label="3m",
                step="month",
                stepmode="backward"
            ),
            dict(
                count=6,
                label="6m",
                step="month",
                stepmode="backward"
            ),
            dict(
                count=1,
                label="YTD",
                step="year",
                stepmode="todate"
            ),
            dict(
                count=1,
                label="1y",
                step="year",
                stepmode="backward"
            ),
            dict(
                step="all",
                label="All"
            )
        ]
    )
)

# ============================================================
# 9. Configurazione Plotly
# ============================================================

config = {
    "displayModeBar": True,
    "displaylogo": False,
    "responsive": True,
    "modeBarButtonsToAdd": [
        "resetScale2d"
    ]
}

# ============================================================
# 10. HTML con selettori data in alto a destra
# ============================================================

plot_div = fig.to_html(
    full_html=False,
    include_plotlyjs="cdn",
    config=config,
    div_id="portwatch_chart"
)

html = f"""
<!DOCTYPE html>
<html>
<head>
    <meta charset="utf-8">
    <title>PortWatch Chart</title>

    <style>
        body {{
            margin: 0;
            padding: 0;
            font-family: Arial, sans-serif;
            background: white;
        }}

        .chart-wrapper {{
            width: 100%;
            position: relative;
            padding-top: 10px;
        }}

        .date-controls {{
            position: absolute;
            top: 23px;
            right: 18px;
            z-index: 1000;
            display: flex;
            align-items: center;
            gap: 8px;
            font-size: 13px;
        }}

        .date-controls input {{
            height: 22px;
            font-size: 13px;
            border: 1px solid #c7c7c7;
            padding: 1px 4px;
            color: #1a4cff;
            background: white;
        }}

        .date-controls button {{
            height: 24px;
            border: none;
            background: transparent;
            cursor: pointer;
            font-size: 13px;
            color: black;
        }}

        .date-controls button:hover {{
            text-decoration: underline;
        }}
    </style>
</head>

<body>

<div class="chart-wrapper">

    <div class="date-controls">
        <input type="date" id="startDate" value="{start_date.strftime('%Y-%m-%d')}">
        <span>—</span>
        <input type="date" id="endDate" value="{end_date.strftime('%Y-%m-%d')}">
        <button id="resetData">Reset Data</button>
    </div>

    {plot_div}

</div>

<script>
    const chart = document.getElementById("portwatch_chart");

    const startInput = document.getElementById("startDate");
    const endInput = document.getElementById("endDate");
    const resetButton = document.getElementById("resetData");

    const originalStart = "{start_date.strftime('%Y-%m-%d')}";
    const originalEnd = "{end_date.strftime('%Y-%m-%d')}";

    function makeWeeklyTicks(startDate, endDate) {{
        const ticks = [];
        const current = new Date(startDate);

        while (current <= endDate) {{
            ticks.push(current.toISOString().slice(0, 10));
            current.setDate(current.getDate() + 7);
        }}

        return ticks;
    }}

    function updateRangeFromInputs() {{
        const start = startInput.value;
        const end = endInput.value;

        if (!start || !end) {{
            return;
        }}

        const startDate = new Date(start);
        const endDate = new Date(end);

        if (startDate > endDate) {{
            return;
        }}

        const ticks = makeWeeklyTicks(startDate, endDate);

        Plotly.relayout(chart, {{
            "xaxis.range": [start, end],
            "xaxis.tickmode": "array",
            "xaxis.tickvals": ticks,
            "xaxis.tickformat": "%-d %b"
        }});
    }}

    startInput.addEventListener("change", updateRangeFromInputs);
    endInput.addEventListener("change", updateRangeFromInputs);

    resetButton.addEventListener("click", function() {{
        startInput.value = originalStart;
        endInput.value = originalEnd;

        const ticks = makeWeeklyTicks(
            new Date(originalStart),
            new Date(originalEnd)
        );

        Plotly.relayout(chart, {{
            "xaxis.range": [originalStart, originalEnd],
            "xaxis.tickmode": "array",
            "xaxis.tickvals": ticks,
            "xaxis.tickformat": "%-d %b"
        }});
    }});

    chart.on("plotly_relayout", function(eventData) {{
        if (eventData["xaxis.range[0]"] && eventData["xaxis.range[1]"]) {{
            startInput.value = eventData["xaxis.range[0]"].slice(0, 10);
            endInput.value = eventData["xaxis.range[1]"].slice(0, 10);
        }}

        if (eventData["xaxis.range"]) {{
            startInput.value = eventData["xaxis.range"][0].slice(0, 10);
            endInput.value = eventData["xaxis.range"][1].slice(0, 10);
        }}
    }});
</script>

</body>
</html>
"""

# ============================================================
# 11. Salvataggio
# ============================================================

output_path = Path(r"C:\Users\MaccagnanG\Downloads\chart_metric_tons_con_datepicker.html")

with open(output_path, "w", encoding="utf-8") as f:
    f.write(html)

fig.show(config=config)

print(f"Grafico HTML salvato in:\n{output_path}")
